code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test18-JAN-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and very low HbA1c, suggesting possible overtreatment of diabetes or malnutrition. Elevated creatinine indicates impaired renal function. Urine findings (proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, abnormal color/appearance) suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or other renal pathology. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation, review of diabetes management, and nephrology evaluation.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
